member.name: Neil O'Brien
member.role: MP for Harborough, Oadby and Wigston
member.synopsis: Neil O'Brien is the Conservative MP for Harborough, Oadby and Wigston , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017. He currently undertakes the role of Shadow Minister (Policy Renewal and Development).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many people were being cared for in forensic support services in each year since 2013-14, broken down by a) high, b) medium and c) low secure services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Approved Premises
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many people were resident in Approved Premises in England and Wales on the most recent date for which data is available, and in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested, which has not previously been published, will be included in the next edition of Offender Accommodation Outcome Statistics, which is due for publication on 30 July. It will be available at the following link: Offender Accommodat...

writtenQuestions.heading: Mentally Disordered Offenders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, with reference to Table 8 of the Restricted Patients Statistics, England and Wales, 2025 release, published on 30 April 2026, of the 297 restricted patients recalled to hospital following conditional discharge, h
writtenQuestions.answerText: Of the 297 recalls of restricted patients to hospital following conditional discharge published in Table 7 (not Table 8), (a) 66 were discharged by the Secretary of State and (b) 231 by the First-tier Tribunal (Mental Health).

writtenQuestions.heading: Home Office: Public Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the 1% capital budget reduction announced in the Defence Investment Plan on her Department's delivery of spending plans; and where those reducti
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office is actively considering how to manage the 1% reduction in 2026-27 and for future years. Our priority will be protecting delivery of the Department’s priority capital projects and programmes.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The department’s core capital priorities remain unchanged. The School Rebuilding Programme continues in full delivery, and funding for school and college maintenance and special educational needs capital is protected. This includes our commitment to remov...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The BBC is operationally independent from the government. As such, decisions on how licence fee income is spent are for the BBC itself.The BBC Group Annual Report and Accounts is laid before Parliament and published annually in accordance with the Royal C...

writtenQuestions.heading: Young People: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what the average hourly earnings by single year of age for those aged (a) 16, (b) 17, (c) 18, (d) 19, (e) 20, (f) 21, (g) 22, (h) 23, (i) 24 and (j) 25 were in each year since 2010.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested falls under the remit of the UK Statistics Authority.A response to the Parliamentary Question of 24th June is attached.

items.text: A member of the advisory board of Onward, a think tank which aims to produce modernising ideas to address some of the biggest challenges facing the UK. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 25 April 2018 (Registered 16 June 2020)
